cot_content:
Okay, let's look at the details of each option to determine which is better for cleaning oily hands with dirt.

Option A suggests using clay dirt. Clay is a type of fine-grained natural rock or soil material that is plastic when wet, but it may not be the best for removing oil. Clay can be sticky and might not absorb oil as effectively. It could potentially leave a residue or not clean as thoroughly.

Option B suggests using gritty loose dirt. Gritty dirt would likely contain more sand or small particles, which can help to scrub away oils through physical abrasion. The description in B also mentions rubbing for a few minutes, which gives more time for the dirt to work on the oil. 

When cleaning oily hands, the key is to use something that can absorb or physically remove the oil. Gritty dirt is more likely to have this scrubbing effect, whereas clay might just smear the oil around. 

So, based on the properties of the dirt types described, Option B seems more effective for cleaning oily hands.
label: majority
